title: Agelanthus gracilis

taxonomy: Agelanthus gracilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as mistletoe. This parasitic plant is native to southern Africa. The Loranthaceae family consists of around 90 genera and approximately 900 species, with Agelanthus gracilis being one of them. Agelanthus gracilis is often found growing on the branches of trees, using its haustorium to extract nutrients from the host plant. Its classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and growth habits.
